commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a TypeScript AST boundary-safety guard and baseline, wires it into `check` and `check:changed`, and hardens selected bounded-response and UTF-16 truncation paths.

PR surface: Source -9, Tests +2173, Config +2, Other +532. Total +2698 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection of current main shows representative raw UTF-16 truncation and direct success-body JSON reads in the touched paths, and the PR adds focused tests for those cases. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the new heuristic guard and MiniMax fail-closed cap, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Add boundary safety guard for response reads and truncation
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a boundary safety guard that inventories existing raw UTF-16 text truncation and unbounded response-body reads, then blocks newly introduced production-file violations in changed-file checks.
- Wire the guard into `pnpm check`, `pnpm check:changed`, and package scripts with a baseline so existing debt stays visible without blocking unrelated PRs.
- Fix the shared response snippet helper and representative ACP/MiniMax/provider call sites to use bounded response reads and UTF-16-safe truncation.

## Origin / follow-up

Follow-up to #97620. That PR fixed one unbounded provider response-read path. This follow-up addresses the recurring root pattern behind the recent boundary-fix cluster by adding a CI guard for new high-confidence response-body and UTF-16 text-truncation bypasses instead of continuing to rely only on one-off call-site fixes.

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Recent boundary fixes have repeatedly patched individual channel/provider call sites for the same two failure classes: user-visible text truncated through raw UTF-16 code units, and external HTTP response bodies read without a byte cap. Without an automated guard, new provider/channel/plugin code can reintroduce the same failure class after the canonical helpers already exist.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause CI/tooling guard for boundary safety regressions, plus small canonical-helper hardening.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local CI/tooling enforcement path and touched helper/call-site regressions. The proof is intentionally scoped to repository guard behavior and local `Response` stream boundaries, not remote service availability.
- **Root cause:** The source invariant was missing from CI: new production code could still bypass OpenClaw's canonical UTF-16 and bounded-response helpers even though those helpers existed. A few shared helpers and representative call sites also still contained raw response reads or raw head truncation, so the repository had no enforced contract that new boundary code must use the canonical owners.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the root/source invariant instead of another sibling symptom because `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` scans production TypeScript with the TypeScript AST, compares findings to a checked-in baseline, and fails only on new changed-file violations. It catches high-confidence raw string head truncation and awaited external `Response` body reads while avoiding tests, fixtures, generated/vendor code, Express response writers, hash/protocol slices, and collection slicing. Baseline identity is stable across line-only movement by comparing file/rule/match counts instead of treating line numbers as part of the violation identity. `scripts/check-changed.mjs` runs the guard for core/extension changes, while `scripts/check.mjs` checks full baseline drift.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change broad runtime behavior, rewrite all legacy baseline entries, add new public APIs, change response-size defaults outside the touched MiniMax success path, or modify credentials/configuration. Full legacy remediation and exhaustive taint analysis are out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` is the source-of-truth validator for new boundary-safety violations; `test/fixtures/boundary-safety-inventory.json` is the baseline contract for current legacy inventory; `@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit` remains the canonical bounded response helper; `src/shared/utf16-slice.ts` remains the canonical UTF-16-safe truncation owner for runtime code.
- **Patch quality notes:** The guard is intentionally high-confidence rather than exhaustive: it prevents new clear bypasses without forcing noisy legacy cleanup. The baseline currently contains 273 known entries on this branch and can be updated only through the explicit `--all --update-baseline` command after intentional migrations. Round 2 addresses ClawSweeper's baseline-identity finding by keeping known findings stable when only their line number changes. Related open PR scan: exact searches for `boundary/response-body-limit`, `boundary/text-utf16-truncation`, and `check-boundary-safety` found no open PR already adding these rule names.
- **Target test file:** `test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts`, `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`, `src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts`, `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts`, and `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** New raw `messageText.slice(0, maxChars - 1)` text truncation is flagged; `truncateUtf16Safe(...)` is accepted; collection/hash/protocol slices are ignored; awaited `response.json()` / `res.text().catch(...)` reads are flagged; Express `res.json({...})` writers are ignored; a baseline-known finding remains accepted when only its line number moves; `--json` mode keeps stdout parseable while routing human status text to stderr; diagnostic snippets cancel exact-cap stalling streams; MiniMax tests now exercise real `Response` bodies for bounded JSON reads; the changed-file plan includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety` for core/extension changes.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression guard targets only high-confidence production-file sinks and leaves existing findings in a baseline, so it protects the source invariant for new code without requiring an unrelated legacy cleanup PR or noisy whole-repo rewrite.

## Root Cause

The root cause is a missing repository-level source invariant for boundary code. User-visible text can still be shortened with raw UTF-16 code-unit slicing, which can leave dangling surrogate halves when the cut lands inside an emoji or other surrogate-pair character. Remote HTTP responses can still be read with `response.json()`, `response.text()`, or `response.arrayBuffer()` directly, which buffers the entire body before OpenClaw can enforce the intended byte budget.

The canonical helpers existed, but the repository did not have a changed-file guard that forced new production code toward those helpers. As a result, each fix landed as a local call-site patch, while the next integration change could reintroduce the same boundary bypass in a sibling area. The shared response snippet path also still carried a raw no-stream fallback and raw character slicing, so even helper consumers were not fully protected.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** New production code that bypasses UTF-16-safe text truncation or bounded response-body helpers is now caught by the changed-file guard, while existing legacy findings remain tracked in a full-repo baseline.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw repair worktree at `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774`, branch `repair/pr-97774`, current PR #97774 head. The exercised behavior is repository CI/tooling enforcement plus local `Response` stream boundary handling.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:boundary-safety:all
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:boundary-safety
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm check:changed --dry-run
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json > /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stdout.txt 2> /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stderr.txt
```

```bash
node -e "const fs=require('node:fs'); const stdout=fs.readFileSync('/tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stdout.txt','utf8'); const stderr=fs.readFileSync('/tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stderr.txt','utf8'); const parsed=JSON.parse(stdout); if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) throw new Error('stdout was not a JSON array'); if (stdout.includes('Boundary safety changed-file check passed')) throw new Error('stdout contained human status text'); if (!stderr.includes('Boundary safety changed-file check passed')) throw new Error('stderr did not contain human status text');"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts -t "truncates parented task progress"
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm tsgo:core
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 && pnpm lint:scripts
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-97774 diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --all
Boundary safety baseline matches (273 entries).
```

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs
Boundary safety changed-file check passed (0 changed-file entries, all baseline-known).
```

```text
$ node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json > /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stdout.txt 2> /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stderr.txt
$ node -e "JSON.parse(stdout) + status-stream assertions"
exit 0; stdout parsed as a JSON array; stdout did not contain human status text; stderr contained `Boundary safety changed-file check passed`.
```

```text
[check:changed:dry-run] lanes=core, coreTests, tooling
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:database-first-legacy-stores
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:media-download-helpers
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:boundary-safety
[check:changed:dry-run] would run: pnpm check:import-cycles
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
```

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards.
packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts: 12 tests passed.
src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts: 1 test passed.
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts -t "truncates parented task progress" exited 0.
```

```text
pnpm tsgo:core exited 0.
pnpm tsgo:core:test exited 0.
pnpm lint:scripts exited 0.
git diff --check exited 0 with no whitespace errors.
```

```text
check-upstream-fixed: CONTINUE_ALLOWED; upstream_fixed=false; stop_required=false; sync_before_fix_required=false.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The full guard baseline matches 273 known legacy entries on the current PR repair worktree, while the changed-file guard passes because the patch itself introduces no new unbaseline-known violations. `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json` now leaves stdout parseable as a JSON array and writes the human success status to stderr. The focused tooling test now includes both the line-shift baseline identity regression and the JSON stdout contract regression. `pnpm check:changed --dry-run` includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety` for this core/tooling change, proving the guard is wired into the changed-file path. The touched helper/call-site regressions verify UTF-16-safe snippet/progress truncation and bounded MiniMax success response reads.
- **What was not tested:** No browser UI or hosted CI run was exercised locally. Remote MiniMax availability is not claimed here; the MiniMax change is covered at the local fetch/`Response` boundary, which is the behavior changed by replacing raw `res.json()` with the bounded JSON reader.

## Review findings addressed

- **Proof request / real behavior evidence:** The reviewer-facing proof is intentionally mapped to the actual behavior changed here: repository guard execution, baseline diffing, and local `Response` stream boundary handling. No browser UI, hosted CI rerun, or remote MiniMax availability is claimed; local chat/RPC/Control UI proof would not exercise the changed CI/tooling boundary more directly than the recorded script runs and local `Response` stream tests.
- **RF-001 — status label:** Expected to clear after this round 2 update is pushed and ClawSweeper re-evaluates the PR; no code change is tied to the label itself.
- **RF-002 — baseline line-shift defect:** Fixed in `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` by comparing baseline entries with stable file/rule/match identity counts instead of line-number-inclusive keys.
- **RF-003 — MiniMax 4 MiB cap policy:** Maintainer decision. The PR body now explicitly calls out that MiniMax VLM successful responses over 4 MiB fail closed via the bounded JSON reader and that this provider-compatibility tradeoff should be maintainer-accepted before merge.
- **RF-004 — unrelated PR block from line shifts:** Fixed by the same stable baseline identity change; a touched file with a baseline-known violation that only moved line numbers no longer appears as a new violation.
- **RF-005 — MiniMax success-response compatibility:** Maintainer decision. This remains intentional fail-closed behavior for unbounded provider success bodies, with the compatibility risk disclosed in Merge risk.
- **RF-006 — repair split:** Fixed the focused script/test baseline-identity issue; the MiniMax cap remains explicitly disclosed as maintainer compatibility policy.
- **RF-007 — stable baseline findings:** Fixed in `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs`; line numbers remain in output/baseline for diagnostics but no longer define violation identity.
- **RF-008 — requested tooling test:** Passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts` with 11 tests.
- **RF-009 — requested JSON check:** Passed: `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json` stdout parses as a JSON array and exits successfully.
- **RF-010 — requested full baseline check:** Passed: `pnpm check:boundary-safety:all` / `node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --all` reported `Boundary safety baseline matches (273 entries).`
- **RF-011 — requested changed dry-run:** Passed: `pnpm check:changed --dry-run` includes `pnpm check:boundary-safety`.
- **RF-012 — JSON stdout contract:** Fixed in `scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs` by routing human status text to stderr when `--json` is active. The focused test and real command parse check now prove stdout stays machine-readable.

## CI follow-up addressed

- **Current-head CI attribution:** The two failing compact checks on head `47caaf2751ad24aafdd02adc853640c8f42b94b3` were current-PR direct regressions, not stale/main-baseline/infra failures. `checks-node-compact-small-whole-1` failed MiniMax/image-tool tests plus an exact-cap PDF error-body test; `checks-node-compact-large-whole-1` failed MiniMax media-understanding tests. Both failure classes line up with this PR's bounded MiniMax JSON reader and response-snippet boundary changes.
- **Fix applied:** MiniMax test fixtures now return real JSON `Response` bodies, with fresh bodies for multi-image calls, so the tests exercise the same bounded `Response.body` path as production. Diagnostic text snippets now cancel exact-cap stalling streams, while `readResponseWithLimit` keeps its success-response overflow semantics.
- **Round 3 verification evidence:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  94 passed (94)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  13 passed (13)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  15 passed (15)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts src/media-understanding/image.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  29 passed (29)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  54 passed (54)
Tests  1024 passed (1024)
```

```text
pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm lint:core
exit 0
```

## Review follow-up addressed

- **ClawSweeper P2 JSON-mode finding:** The guard's `--json` mode no longer mixes human status text into stdout. JSON inventory output remains on stdout; success/update-baseline status text goes to stderr when `--json` is active.
- **Round 4 verification evidence:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
```

```text
node scripts/check-boundary-safety.mjs --json > /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stdout.txt 2> /tmp/openclaw-boundary-json-stderr.txt
JSON.parse(stdout) succeeded; stdout did not contain human status text; stderr contained Boundary safety changed-file check passed.
```

```text
pnpm check:boundary-safety && pnpm check:boundary-safety:all
Boundary safety changed-file check passed (0 changed-file entries, all baseline-known).
Boundary safety baseline matches (273 entries).
```

```text
pnpm lint:scripts
pnpm check:changed --dry-run
pnpm tsgo:core:test
git diff --check
all exited 0
```

## Regression Test Plan

- `test/scripts/check-boundary-safety.test.ts` locks guard detection and false-positive boundaries for string truncation, response-body reads, Express writers, collection slices, baseline diffing, line-shift-stable baseline identity, and parseable `--json` stdout.
- `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.test.ts` locks bounded response snippet behavior, UTF-16-safe snippet truncation, and exact-cap diagnostic stream cancellation.
- `src/agents/minimax-vlm.boundary.test.ts` locks success-response overflow cancellation.
- `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts` and `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` lock MiniMax callers against real bounded JSON `Response` bodies instead of `.json()`-only mocks.
- `src/agents/tools/pdf-native-providers.test.ts` locks exact-cap provider error-body cancellation.
- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` locks parented ACP task progress truncation without dangling surrogate halves.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** CI/tooling, boundary safety, provider response reads, UTF-16 text truncation.
- **Risk explanation:** The guard can block new changed-file code that uses raw response reads or raw string truncation in production files. It should not block unrelated legacy debt because existing entries are baseline-known by stable file/rule/match identity, not by fragile line numbers, and it intentionally excludes tests/fixtures/generated/vendor files plus common false positives. MiniMax VLM success responses over 4 MiB now fail closed through the bounded provider JSON reader instead of fully buffering the body; that provider-compatibility tradeoff is intentional and should be maintainer-accepted before merge.
- **Why acceptable:** The failure mode is actionable: use `truncateUtf16Safe`, `sliceUtf16Safe`, `readResponseWithLimit`, `readResponseTextSnippet`, `readProviderJsonResponse`, or add a narrow `boundary-safety-ignore <rule-id>: <reason>` for a proven false positive. This turns a repeated review/bug pattern into a local CI failure before PR submission.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not attempt full legacy remediation, exhaustive taint analysis, or live third-party provider/channel validation.
